{"id":"cm-2j5.2","title":"A.2: Verify context-mode tools work end-to-end in OMP","description":"## What

Integration test: run real workloads through context-mode from an OMP session to verify all tools work correctly with Qwen models.

## Test Cases

1. execute(language: "shell", code: "git log --oneline -50") — verify output stays in sandbox
2. execute_file(path: large_file, language: "python", code: analysis) — verify FILE_CONTENT injection
3. batch_execute with 5 commands + 5 queries — verify indexed search works
4. fetch_and_index(url) — verify web content indexing
5. index(path: local_file) → search(queries: [...]) — verify FTS5 round-trip

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 test cases produce correct results from OMP
- [ ] Token consumption measured and compared to raw tool usage
- [ ] No errors from better-sqlite3 FTS5 in OMP's Node/Bun runtime
- [ ] Stats endpoint reports accurate savings","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"noreply@anthropic.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:36:26Z","created_by":"lora_forge/crew/forge","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:36:26Z"}

{"id":"cm-2j5.3","title":"B.1: System prompt for Qwen context-mode tool routing","description":"## What

Create system prompt instructions that teach Qwen models when and how to use context-mode tools instead of raw shell/file tools. Equivalent of the context-mode skill that Claude Code uses.

## Problem

Claude Code routes to context-mode because:
1. The context-mode skill defines when to use each tool
2. PreToolUse hooks emit "CONTEXT TIP" reminders
3. Claude's training makes it good at tool routing

Qwen needs explicit, structured instructions to achieve similar routing behavior.

## Approach

- Adapt the context-mode skill (skills/context-mode/SKILL.md) for OMP's prompt format
- Create decision tree: "if output might be >20 lines, use execute instead of raw tool"
- Include language selection guide (JS for APIs, Python for data, shell for commands)
- Add examples specific to Gas Town workflows (bd commands, git operations, pipeline runs)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] System prompt fits within OMP's prompt budget
- [ ] Qwen routes >80% of large-output commands through context-mode (manual review of 20 sessions)
- [ ] No false positives on small commands (git add, mkdir, etc.)
- [ ] Instructions work for both Qwen3-max and Qwen3-coder-plus","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"noreply@anthropic.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:36:39Z","created_by":"lora_forge/crew/forge","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:36:39Z"}

new_value: Created .omp/skills/context-mode/SKILL.md with decision tree, tool reference, usage guidelines, and anti-patterns adapted for Qwen models.

old_value:
{"id":"cm-2j5.4","title":"B.2: OMP hook for context-mode tool routing reminders","description":"## What
Create an OMP lifecycle hook that reminds the agent to use context-mode tools for large-output operations, equivalent to Claude Code's PreToolUse hook that injects "CONTEXT TIP" reminders.

## Why
Claude Code's context-mode plugin uses a PreToolUse hook that fires before Bash/Read/cat calls and injects a reminder to use execute/execute_file instead. OMP doesn't have PreToolUse hooks — it uses a different lifecycle event system (session_start, before_agent_start, tool_call, etc.). We need an equivalent mechanism.

## Approach
1. Identify OMP's tool_call lifecycle hook (fires before tool execution)
2. Create a hook that pattern-matches on tool names likely to produce large output (shell commands with git/curl/cat/find, file reads on large files)
3. Inject a reminder into the agent's context suggesting context-mode alternatives
4. Keep the hook lightweight — false negatives are acceptable, false positives waste tokens

## Inputs
- OMP hook system at oh_my_pi/mayor/rig/packages/coding-agent/src/hooks/
- Context-mode PreToolUse hook pattern from .claude/settings.json

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hook fires on shell/file-read tool calls in OMP sessions
- [ ] Reminder suggests specific context-mode tool alternatives
- [ ] No measurable latency impact on tool execution (<10ms)
- [ ] Hook is configurable (can be disabled per-session)
- [ ] Works with both Qwen3-max and Qwen3-coder-plus","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"noreply@anthropic.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:39:29Z","created_by":"lora_forge/crew/forge","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:39:29Z"}

new_value: Created .omp/hooks/context-mode-routing.ts with tool_call event handler. Intercepts bash commands matching large-output patterns (git log/diff, test runners, curl, grep, bd/gt commands) and file reads. Sends steering messages via pi.sendMessage with context-mode alternatives. Skips allowlisted commands (mutations, small output). Uses OMP hook API pattern from permission-gate.ts example.

old_value:
{"id":"cm-2j5.5","title":"C.1: Persistent FTS5 knowledge base across sessions","description":"## What
Modify context-mode's ContentStore to support named persistent databases that survive across polecat sessions, enabling knowledge reuse.

## Why
Currently ContentStore creates an ephemeral in-memory FTS5 database per session. When a polecat indexes documentation or codebase structure, that knowledge is lost when the session ends. The next polecat on the same convoy wastes tokens re-indexing the same content. Persistent databases let polecats inherit indexed knowledge.

## Approach
1. Add a `database` parameter to ContentStore initialization (default: ":memory:" for backward compat)
2. When a named database is specified, use a file-backed SQLite DB at a known path (e.g., `.claude/context-mode/<name>.db`)
3. Add `list_databases` and `delete_database` tools for management
4. Add a `database` parameter to index/search tools to select which DB to query
5. Handle concurrent access safely (SQLite WAL mode)

## Inputs
- ContentStore in context_mode/mayor/rig/src/content-store.ts
- better-sqlite3 documentation for WAL mode and file-backed DBs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Named persistent databases created at configurable path
- [ ] Default behavior unchanged (ephemeral in-memory DB)
- [ ] Indexed content survives process restart when using named DB
- [ ] WAL mode enabled for concurrent read safety
- [ ] list_databases returns available persistent DBs with sizes
- [ ] delete_database removes DB file and confirms
- [ ] Search across multiple databases in single query
- [ ] No performance regression for in-memory mode","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"noreply@anthropic.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T03:39:38Z","created_by":"lora_forge/crew/forge","updated_at":"2026-03-02T03:39:38Z"}

new_value: Implemented persistent FTS5 knowledge bases. store.ts: ContentStore.openNamed(), listPersistent(), deletePersistent() with file-backed SQLite at ~/.claude/context-mode/<name>.db. server.ts: resolveStore(database?) routing, database param on index/search/batch_execute, new list_databases and delete_database tools. Committed as 42b4aa0.
